factors.summary: Demand is accelerating due to strong revenue growth and increased volume.

evidence.text: During the second quarter of 2018, our business units produced strong revenue growth across all three segments. Average daily package volume increased 3.0% for the second quarter of 2018 and 3.8% year-to-date.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating with strong revenue growth and increased volume.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with evidence of pricing initiatives and base rate increases.

evidence.text: Overall revenue per piece increased 3.6% for the second quarter of 2018 (3.0% year-to-date) compared with the same period of 2017 and was impacted by changes in base rates, customer and product mix and fuel surcharge rates.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully raising prices, evidenced by base rate increases.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward lower-margin segments, particularly SurePost, which is affecting margins.

evidence.text: Ground revenue per piece increased for the second quarter and year-to-date periods of 2018, primarily due to base rate increases and higher fuel surcharge rates. These factors were partially offset by changes in customer and product mix, as we experienced faster volume growth in our SurePost product and a decrease in average billable weight per piece.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, affecting margins.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with strong performance across all segments.

evidence.text: Consolidated revenue increased 9.6% to $17.456 billion for the second quarter of 2018 when compared to 2017. For the year-to-date period, consolidated revenue increased 10.0% to $34.569 billion in 2018 from $31.437 billion in 2017.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating strongly across all segments.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across all segments, driven by strong demand.

evidence.text: Average daily package volume increased 3.0% for the second quarter of 2018 and 3.8% year-to-date. Total Avg. Daily Package Volume increased 5.4% for the second quarter and 5.0% year-to-date.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across all segments, driven by strong demand.

factors.summary: The fastest-growing end markets include retail, healthcare, and automotive, with strong demand across these sectors.

evidence.text: Growth was focused within the retail, healthcare, manufacturing and automotive industry verticals. Business-to-consumer shipments, which represented approximately 49% of the total U.S. Domestic Package volume for the quarter, grew 7% (up 8.7% year-to-date) and drove overall increases in both air and ground shipments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong demand in key end markets like retail, healthcare, and automotive.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are rising and impacting margins.

evidence.text: Total cost per piece increased 8.1% for the second quarter of 2018 compared with the same period of 2017 (6.8% year-to-date) which includes transformation strategy costs of $196 million. The cost per piece increase was primarily driven by costs related to the improvement of our air and ground networks, including additional aircraft leases to improve our air service reliability, costs related to the implementation of Saturday operations in additional markets, new facility and technology projects and higher pension expense. Costs were negatively impacted by rising fuel prices.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs, particularly fuel, are rising and impacting margins.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to volume growth, wage increases, and pension expenses.

evidence.text: Higher employee compensation and benefit costs largely resulting from volume growth, which impacted an increase in average daily union labor hours (up 5.8%), scheduled union pay rate and benefit increases and growth in the overall size of the workforce due to facility expansions. Labor hour increases were also related to the significant expansion in Saturday operations. In addition, pension expense increased due to lower year end discount rates used to measure the pension benefit obligation, driving higher service costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to volume growth, wage increases, and pension expenses.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to higher costs and transformation strategy expenses.

evidence.text: Operating margin decreased 380 basis points to 9.1% (down 320 basis points to 8.2% year-to-date). Without the impact of transformation strategy costs, operating profit decreased $120 million for the second quarter and $314 million year-to-date with operating margins decreasing 190 basis points and 220 basis points, respectively.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are decreasing due to higher costs and transformation strategy expenses.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious but confident, with investments in network improvements and technology.

evidence.text: The benefits of our efficiency and growth initiatives in the U.S. will not be fully realized until future periods. We believe this adjusted information provides useful comparison of year-to-year ongoing operating performance without considering the short-term impact of transformation strategy costs. We evaluate the performance of our businesses on an adjusted basis.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is investing in network improvements and technology, indicating confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include rising fuel prices and labor costs, which are weighing on profits.

evidence.text: Higher purchased transportation costs due to volume growth and an increase in employee benefit costs driven by lower pension discount rates further weighed on profits. Additionally, operating profit was negatively impacted by costs related to continued investments in our air and ground networks, including implementation of Saturday operations in additional markets and new facility and technology projects.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising fuel and labor costs are negatively impacting profits.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction focused on efficiency and growth initiatives.

evidence.text: The benefits of our efficiency and growth initiatives in the U.S. will not be fully realized until future periods. We believe this adjusted information provides useful comparison of year-to-year ongoing operating performance without considering the short-term impact of transformation strategy costs. We evaluate the performance of our businesses on an adjusted basis.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear long-term strategic focus on efficiency and growth.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and foreign exchange movements are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: Fuel Surcharges: UPS applies a fuel surcharge on our domestic air and ground services. The air fuel surcharge is based on the U.S. Department of Energy’s (“DOE”) Gulf Coast spot price for a gallon of kerosene-type jet fuel, while the ground fuel surcharge is based on the DOE’s On-Highway Diesel Fuel price. Currency Benefit / (Cost) – (in millions)*: Net of currency hedging; amount represents the change in currency translation compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Fuel prices and foreign exchange movements pose risks to near-term results.

factors.summary: New fuel surcharge changes and the expansion of Saturday operations are catalysts for improved results.

evidence.text: Effective June 11, 2018, the Ground Fuel Surcharge increased by 0.50% for all thresholds. These surcharges will continue to be based on the national U.S. Average On Highway Diesel Fuel price and adjusted weekly. A Saturday pickup stop charge went into effect on May 1, 2017 and varies depending on the pickup service selected.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New fuel surcharge changes and Saturday operations are expected to improve results.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a tailwind with strong demand, particularly in e-commerce.

evidence.text: Growth was focused within the retail, healthcare, manufacturing and automotive industry verticals. Business-to-consumer shipments, which represented approximately 49% of the total U.S. Domestic Package volume for the quarter, grew 7% (up 8.7% year-to-date) and drove overall increases in both air and ground shipments.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong economic demand, particularly in e-commerce, is a tailwind.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting results, particularly in the International Package segment.

evidence.text: Currency Benefit / (Cost) – (in millions)*: Net of currency hedging; amount represents the change in currency translation compared to the prior year. Revenue: The change in overall revenue was impacted by the following factors in 2018 compared with the corresponding period of 2017: Volume, Rates / Product Mix, Fuel Surcharge, Currency, Total Revenue Change.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements are impacting results, particularly in the International segment.
